---
title: "High Performance Techniques!"
subtitle: "CCP-TEPP SummerSchool 2026"
author: "Andrew Lister"
institute: "UKRI - STFC"
format:
  revealjs:
    navigation-mode: vertical
    theme: serif
toc: true
toc-depth: 1
toc-title: Outline

transition: fade

from: markdown+emoji
---

# Preamble

## Disclaimers

This is a scattershot of content from the following (in development courses) with some additions...

- [HPC Advanced User](https://carpentries-incubator.github.io/HPC_Advanced_User-Teaching-performance-programming-to-scientists/index.html)
- [Using High-Performance Computing Systems](https://carpentries-incubator.github.io/hpc-intro/index.html)

## Goals!

- Understand how language choice and platform affect decisions in implementation
- Run an mpi code to see Amdahls law in action
- Know what strong and weak scaling mean

{{< include cache.qmd-extra >}}
{{< include scaling.qmd-extra >}}
{{< include mixed_precision.qmd-extra >}}

# Final comments

- Check that arrays are accessed contiguously
- Parallelism can only help with the parallel bits!
- If you don't need the accuracy, don't compute it
